data.current_summary: Get logs for a cron job
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve execution logs for a cron job
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves execution logs for a specified cron job. Requires the cron job ID as input. Returns a collection of log entries containing execution timestamps, status, output, and error information. Logs are typically ordered chronologically and may be paginated based on query parameters.
data.reasoning: The operation lacks a description entirely, making it difficult for AI agents to understand what inputs are required, what data is returned, or how to use it effectively. The summary is generic. Added details about required job ID parameter, log entry contents (timestamps, status, output, errors), and pagination context to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the timezone setting for the authenticated account. Returns the timezone identifier (e.g., 'UTC', 'America/New_York') used for scheduling and displaying cron job execution times. This setting affects how cron job schedules are interpreted and when jobs are executed.
data.reasoning: The operation has no description, leaving AI agents uncertain about the return format, purpose, and impact on cron job scheduling. Added context about timezone format, its role in job scheduling, and how it affects execution times to improve clarity and AI agent decision-making.

data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing cron job's configuration with up to 16 parameters including schedule, URL, HTTP method, headers, and execution settings. Changes take effect immediately for future executions.

message: Operation 'enableCronJob' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear but description is missing. Added context about what 'enable' means (allows execution), required inputs, authentication, return value, side effect (job begins executing), and idempotency note for AI agent clarity.

data.description_suggestion: Enables a previously disabled cron job, allowing it to execute on its configured schedule. Requires job ID and API token authentication. Returns the updated CronJob object with enabled status. The job will begin executing at its next scheduled time. This operation has no effect if the job is already enabled.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear but description is missing. Added context about what 'enable' means (allows execution), required inputs, authentication, return value, side effect (job begins executing), and idempotency note for AI agent clarity.

message: Operation 'disableCronJob' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear but description is missing. Added context about what 'disable' means (prevents execution), required inputs, authentication, return value, side effect (pending executions cancelled), and idempotency note for AI agent clarity.

data.description_suggestion: Disables an active cron job, preventing it from executing on its configured schedule. Requires job ID and API token authentication. Returns the updated CronJob object with disabled status. Any pending executions are cancelled. This operation has no effect if the job is already disabled.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear but description is missing. Added context about what 'disable' means (prevents execution), required inputs, authentication, return value, side effect (pending executions cancelled), and idempotency note for AI agent clarity.

message: Operation 'deleteCronJob' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear but description is missing. Added critical context about permanence (irreversible), required inputs, authentication, return value, and side effects (history deletion, pending execution cancellation) to help AI agents understand the destructive nature of this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a cron job and removes all associated execution history and logs. Requires job ID and API token authentication. Returns a success confirmation. This operation is irreversible - the job and its historical data cannot be recovered. Any pending executions are cancelled immediately.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear but description is missing. Added critical context about permanence (irreversible), required inputs, authentication, return value, and side effects (history deletion, pending execution cancellation) to help AI agents understand the destructive nature of this operation.
